reasoning: The resolution criteria require more than 10 times the baseline number of protests in a 30-day window. The baseline is currently 1.000, which means we would need at least 11 protest events recorded by ACLED within 30 days. Extensive searches for Dec 2025 to Jan 2026 indicate absolutely zero ongoing or newly planned protests, strikes, or widespread demonstrations in Estonia. Historically, generating 11 distinct ACLED protest events in Estonia within a single month requires an intense, nationwide movement (such as the widespread January 2024 teachers' strike). In November 2025, a trade union protested variable-hour contracts, but this was a localized single event outside the Riigikogu and did not escalate into a larger wave as of January 2026. Therefore, for the 30-day period ending January 11, 2026, it is highly improbable that 11 events will accumulate with only 7 days remaining and no signs of unrest. For the subsequent period ending February 3, 2026, the probability remains very low given the stability of the political climate and absence of any scheduled mass strikes or demonstrations. I constrain probabilities near the floor of standard bounds (0.05-0.06).
